definition: Forward guidance transmission is attenuated by approximately 30-50% under positive correlation. Under negative correlation, dovish guidance eases through both bond and equity channels simultaneously (amplifying 1.5-2x); under positive correlation, channels partially offset.
evidence: Del Negro, Giannoni & Patterson (2023) estimate 30-50% attenuation
R-squared of FOMC-day yield changes on equity returns declined from 0.15-0.25 to 0.05-0.10
Bond channel still functions mechanically — attenuation, not collapse
Original 'near-zero' claim from rates_strategist_02 refuted in pair_2 debate
open_questions: Whether attenuation is stable or varies with correlation magnitude
Implications for next easing cycle effectiveness
